div.h2: Padding Algorithm Decision Tree: One Block vs Two Blocks

div.p: STEP 1: Append marker byte used = len + 1 Write 0x80 at data[len] (remaining bytes zeroed by memset)
STEP 2: Can length field fit? Is used ≤ 56? (i.e., ≥ 8 bytes remain in current 64-byte block)

tr.td: One extra input byte (len=55 → len=56) adds an entire extra 64-byte block.
The condition used > 56 (equivalently len ≥ 56) triggers the two-block path.

div.p: STEP 3a: Zero-fill memset(data + used, 0, 56 − used) Fill bytes [used .. 55] with 0x00 Zero-fill count = 56 − used bytes (may be 0 bytes if used = 56, i.e. len = 55)
STEP 4a: Write length field data[56..63] = 64-bit big-endian len × 8 Eight bytes, MSB first: data[56] = bit_len >> 56 ··· data[63] = bit_len & 0xFF
RESULT: 1 BLOCK total_padded = 64 num_blocks = 1 Block 0: data[0..63] ───────────────────────── [msg][0x80][zeros][len×8]
STEP 3b: Zero-fill remainder of Block 0 memset(data + used, 0, 64 − used) Fill bytes [used .. 63] with 0x00 Zero-fill count = 64 − used bytes (Block 0 is now complete but has no length field)
STEP 3c: Zero-fill Block 1 bytes 0–55 memset(data + 64, 0, 56) Bytes [64 .. 119] = 0x00 (length field goes at end of Block 1)
STEP 4b: Write length field in Block 1 data[120..127] = 64-bit big-endian len × 8 data[120] = bit_len >> 56 ··· data[127] = bit_len & 0xFF
RESULT: 2 BLOCKS total_padded = 128 num_blocks = 2 Block 0: data[0..63] Block 1: data[64..127] ───────────────────────────────────────── [msg (0..55)][0x80][zeros] · [zeros (56 bytes)][len×8]
